run_summary: Portfolio holds two 1-day-old positions (MASI, AYI) with minor unrealized moves. New flag batch contains six names spanning defense/geopolitical beta, semicap momentum, airline fuel relief, and a repeat MASI flag. Track record shows 51% overall hit rate (53% at conf4, 47% at conf3); OVERDONE calls have been marginal (50%) while UNDERDONE calls are stronger (56%). Decision: hold both positions (intact theses), buy ALK on UNDERDONE conviction (Iran deal resolution tilts airline fuel economics), watch AVAV (re-entry bar unmet), skip VECO/KTOS/MRCY/MASI as either momentum fades or already flagged/held.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Iran deal-signing headline + broader defense weakness (ITA -0.95% on risk-on day)
entries.thesis: AVAV's drone-heavy revenue mix is disproportionately exposed to active conflict demand. With Trump admin signaling Iran deal signing 'in coming days' and ITA underperforming, the de-rating could extend if a deal actually prints. No 8-K signal so this is sector/macro driven, not company-specific — but the divergence vs. a risk-on tape is notable.
entries.what_kills: Iran deal falls through (Trump-admin source said 'not 100% certain'); AVAV announces a new DoD contract; broader defense bounces as buyers treat dip as opportunity
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning applies: you closed AVAV 10 days ago for a -5.31% loss, citing thesis as BROKEN because the move had extended to 30%+ on no new catalyst. Current flag is UNDERDONE conf 3 citing identical Iran deal de-escalation catalyst. Re-entry discipline requires: (1) confidence >= 4 (this flag is conf 3, so execution layer will downgrade BUY to WATCH anyway), AND (2) genuinely new information not present at exit. The new information here is NEGATIVE: five-day move is now -8.25% vs. prior +30.83%. This means the thesis was right on the fade and has already played out. The current setup is no longer a 'defense de-rating from peace deal' but rather 'defense already sold off, now at support.' That is a different trade (potential bounce) but the original UNDERDONE label does not reflect the new reality. Confidence is 3, re-entry bar is 4. WATCH and wait for either: (a) evidence of a new contract win that would be genuinely new information, or (b) confidence to rise to 4 on fresh evidence.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: No identified company-specific catalyst; semicap sympathy with SMH leadership
entries.thesis: VECO has run ~32% in 5 days with no 8-K, no earnings, and only ~1.4x average volume today. The move appears to be momentum/sympathy with broader semicap strength (SMH +1.7%, ONTO/AEIS also extended). Magnitude on a small-float name without identifiable fundamental catalyst sets up fade risk if the SMH momentum cools.
entries.what_kills: Veeco files an 8-K with a contract win or guidance raise; sell-side upgrade with specific revenue thesis; advanced packaging demand commentary from a primary like AVGO/TSM
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3: VECO has run 32.56% in 5 days with no 8-K, no earnings, and only 1.36x volume. Thesis is momentum/sympathy with semicap leadership (SMH +1.7%, ONTO/AEIS extended). Small-float names without identified fundamental catalysts extending 30%+ runs are textbook momentum plays. This is a fade setup, not a buy setup. However, the execution would be SHORT or SKIP, not BUY. Confidence in OVERDONE label is 3. Track record: OVERDONE at conf 3 has hit only 47%, materially below the 51% baseline. No catalyst_url provided (red flag per instructions). Without a named company-specific catalyst or 8-K, this fails the bar for either CONVICTION or EXPLORATORY entry. SKIP.

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Trump's claim of Iran deal 'subject to finalization' → oil lower → airline fuel relief
entries.thesis: Iran deal framing → oil down ~2% → airline fuel cost relief, layered on risk-on tape and IWM strength. ALK at ~$5B cap is a smaller airline with high fuel sensitivity. If the Iran deal actually finalizes in 'next few days' as Trump claims, oil leg-down extends and ALK's fuel cost tailwind compounds. The move is large but airline operating leverage to fuel is enormous — could underextend if crude breaks lower.
entries.what_kills: Iran deal fails to materialize and oil reverses; or an ALK-specific 8-K surfaces showing a labor cost or operational issue that re-frames this as a relief bounce off a prior decline.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The Iran deal is framed as imminent ('coming days') but Trump's exact language ('subject to finalization') is equivocal and does not constitute a catalyst with high probability of near-term completion. Bear case: The bull thesis rests entirely on oil continuing lower into deal finalization within 3-5 days. However, Trump's statement 'subject to finalization in coming days' is notably non-committal phrasing—'subject to finalization' signals ongoing negotiation, not a closed deal. Geopolitical deal language is routinely aspirational; final execution often stalls for weeks or fails entirely. The 11.95% one-day move and 9.3% five-day move are already substantial, suggesting the market has front-run much of the optimism. If the deal slips past the 3-5 day window (the stated time_horizon), the fuel-cost tailwind thesis collapses and ALK faces mean reversion into a crowded trade, amplified by the fact that the move is already pronounced relative to the strength of the catalyst anchor. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: UNDERDONE conf 3 on airline fuel-cost relief thesis. Iran deal resolution (Trump: 'subject to finalization in coming days') → oil down ~2% → fuel cost tailwind for high-fuel-beta airlines. ALK is a smaller airline (~$5B cap) with disproportionate leverage to crude moves. Move is +11.95% one-day, +9.3% five-day on 1.18x volume — large but justified by operating leverage: 1 dollar/barrel crude swing can swing airline EBIT by ~10-15% annually for smaller carriers. The thesis is not momentum; it's macro-driven. Setup: oil continues lower into deal finalization, ALK's fuel cost relief compounds. What kills: deal fails and oil reverses, or ALK-specific operational/labor issue surfaces. Track record: UNDERDONE at conf 3 has hit 56% (above baseline 51%). However, conviction sizing (15-20% at conf 3 = 15%) is warranted because the mechanism is clear, the horizon ('days') is tight, and the catalyst has a specific date anchor ('coming days'). If deal finalizes within 3-5 days, airlines compound the relief and extend. If deal fails, loss is contained to days-horizon reversal. Sized at 15% conviction on conf 3.)

red_team.weakest_link: The Iran deal is framed as imminent ('coming days') but Trump's exact language ('subject to finalization') is equivocal and does not constitute a catalyst with high probability of near-term completion.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis rests entirely on oil continuing lower into deal finalization within 3-5 days. However, Trump's statement 'subject to finalization in coming days' is notably non-committal phrasing—'subject to finalization' signals ongoing negotiation, not a closed deal. Geopolitical deal language is routinely aspirational; final execution often stalls for weeks or fails entirely. The 11.95% one-day move and 9.3% five-day move are already substantial, suggesting the market has front-run much of the optimism. If the deal slips past the 3-5 day window (the stated time_horizon), the fuel-cost tailwind thesis collapses and ALK faces mean reversion into a crowded trade, amplified by the fact that the move is already pronounced relative to the strength of the catalyst anchor.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.catalyst: Defense sympathy on Iran headlines + IWM strength
entries.thesis: KTOS up 7% but five_day_change is -7.3% — this looks like a bounce off a defense pullback rather than fresh strength. The puzzling part: if Iran is settling, the geopolitical premium that drove defense names higher should unwind, not extend. ITA +5% suggests the market is reading 'Trump shows force → deals get done → more defense spend, not less,' but that read may not hold once the deal language firms up. KTOS is high-beta defense; the move likely overshoots and fades when the de-escalation reality sinks in.
entries.what_kills: Deal falls through and conflict escalates, in which case KTOS extends. Or DoD budget news / specific Kratos contract award surfaces that justifies the move on fundamentals not geopolitics.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3, setup mismatch. KTOS is up 7.22% one-day but -7.29% five-day, meaning it bounced off a larger defense pullback. The thesis is that rallying defense names into Iran de-escalation is a sucker trade — the geopolitical premium should unwind, not extend, once deal language firms up. That is a coherent fade thesis, but the current flag is UNDERDONE-labeled with a BUY recommendation. The contradiction suggests the analyst flagged it as a setup to buy, but the setup is actually best played as a fade (short or SKIP). KTOS has no catalyst_url; the 'ITA strength' is real but sector-level, not company-specific. Track record: OVERDONE at conf 3 is 47% (weak). No 8-K, no contract award, no guidance — only sector sympathy. Without a fresh catalyst unique to KTOS, this fails the bar. SKIP.

entries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
entries.catalyst: Defense sympathy + risk-on tape; no company-specific catalyst
entries.thesis: MRCY up nearly 12% on 1.25x volume with no SEC filing and no earnings catalyst — yet 5-day change is barely positive. That pattern says the move is concentrated entirely in today's session and likely driven by a short squeeze or sympathy with ITA strength, not fundamentals. Defense small-caps with high short interest often spike sharply on risk-on days then fade. Without a confirming catalyst this looks like a momentum/squeeze move that overshoots.
entries.what_kills: A Mercury-specific contract win or analyst upgrade surfaces; or short interest data shows the move was structurally supported by genuine new buyers, not squeeze mechanics.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3: Mercury is up 11.71% on 1.25x volume with no 8-K, no earnings, and five-day change of only +1.27%. That concentration of the entire move into one day, with flat five-day context, is a classic squeeze or momentum pattern in small-cap defense names. Thesis: ITA strength provides cover for a short squeeze; once ITA rolls, MRCY gives back disproportionately. No company-specific catalyst (catalyst_url is null). Track record: OVERDONE at conf 3 is 47% hit rate (weak), and this setup is the weakest version of OVERDONE — single-day gap with no fundamental anchor. The entire gain is vulnerable to mean-reversion if sector momentum stalls. SKIP. This is a short or avoid, not a buy.

entries.name: Masimo Corporation
entries.catalyst: Multi-item 8-K consistent with closing of a previously-telegraphed divestiture or spin-off
entries.thesis: The 8-K bundles Item 1.02 (terminated agreement), 2.01 (completed disposition), 3.01 (delisting/listing rule), 3.03 (security holder rights modification), 5.01 (change in control), 5.02 (officer/director change), and 5.03 (charter amendment). That combination is the fingerprint of a closed spin-off or divestiture of the consumer/Sound United business — long anticipated by activists. Stock flat on triple volume suggests the market views the transaction terms as in-line with what was already priced in. Magnitude (zero move) is rational given the event was telegraphed.
entries.what_kills: If the 8-K reveals an unexpected dilutive financing, a surprise CEO/leadership shake-up, or terms materially worse than expected, the flat tape becomes the mispricing and downside opens up.
entries.reasoning: MASI is already held in the portfolio (RATIONAL conf 4, 8-K divestiture/spin thesis). New flag re-confirms the same 8-K catalyst and rational entry. Stock is flat on 3.17x volume, confirming that the market already priced in the transaction terms. Thesis status: intact, already in position, horizon still open (days). Adding to the position is NOT warranted because: (1) no new information has emerged since entry 1 day ago, (2) thesis has not strengthened (stock is flat), (3) position is already sized at ~24% of equity (11.5465 shares × $179.95 = $2,077 of $10,000 = 20.8%, close to 25% limit). The thesis is intact but the momentum/confirmation is absent. SKIP the add and hold the existing position.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semicap sector strength tied to AI/HBM packaging narrative
entries.thesis: ONTO has run ~28% in 5 days alongside VECO and AEIS. Unlike VECO, ONTO has direct advanced packaging/inspection exposure tied to AI-driven HBM demand, which has been a documented bullish theme. Today's move is consistent with sector beta on a strong SMH day. No 8-K, no obvious mispricing direction.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc.
entries.thesis: AEIS filed an 8-K today with items 8.01 (other events) and 9.01 (exhibits). Item 8.01 is a catch-all that could be anything from a press release to a minor disclosure — without reading the exhibit, the content is opaque. The stock is up ~4% today and ~20% in 5 days. Direction of mispricing cannot be determined without knowing the 8.01 content.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Risk-on tape + housing/builder bid; older 5.02 filing context
entries.thesis: BLDR has a recent 5.02 (officer/director departure or appointment) filing from 2026-06-05 — six days before today's move. The move is occurring on below-average volume (0.69x) into a strong risk-on tape with rate-sensitive names bid. The 5.02 by itself doesn't tell us whether the change was bullish (new CEO, market liked it) or bearish (CFO departure, would weigh on stock). Hard to commit a direction without reading the filing text.
entries.what_kills: Today's move is purely beta to IWM/housing tape and the 5.02 is irrelevant noise.

entries.name: Insmed Incorporated
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no SEC filing or news in our feed
entries.thesis: No filings, no news, modest 1.3x volume on a -7% day with XBI -1.9%. Move is roughly 4x the sector beta but I can't articulate a specific bull or bear thesis without knowing the cause. Could be brensocatib commercial concern, a clinical leak, or a single fund unwind.
entries.what_kills: If a clean explanation appears (broker conference comment, secondary offering rumor), the call should be re-evaluated immediately.

entries.name: WEX Inc.
entries.thesis: WEX -7.4% on essentially average volume (1.02x), no 8-K, no news. WEX has dual exposure to fleet fuel cards (oil sensitivity, but generally positive when prices rise via spread) and corporate travel payments. Direction of oil sensitivity is ambiguous — historically WEX has been a beneficiary of higher fuel prices, not a victim. Can't construct a clean conf-3 thesis either direction.
entries.what_kills: Tape stabilizes and stock recovers — suggests illiquid mid-cap algo overshoot.

entries.name: Pool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Apparent rotation into rate-sensitive / housing complex ahead of CPI
entries.thesis: POOL up ~6% on 1.7x volume with no 8-K and no news. Likely tied to the rotation into housing/rate-sensitive names (XLRE +2.13%) on softer rate expectations into tomorrow's CPI. BLDR also up 6% supports housing-rotation thesis. This is more of a thematic move than a stock-specific mispricing — UNCLEAR because magnitude (6%+) seems large for pure rotation absent a specific catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Hot CPI print tomorrow would unwind the rate-sensitive rotation and these names likely give back gains

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: Semi sector sympathy/catch-up trade; no company-specific signal
entries.thesis: LSCC was down 5-day before today and is closing the gap with the SMH rally. Move is consistent with short-covering on a low-volatility risk-on day rather than a fundamental catalyst — no 8-K, no earnings scheduled. The fact it underperformed the 5-day window while peers rallied suggests today is mean-reversion within the sector.
entries.what_kills: If it fades intraday tomorrow back to flat vs SMH — confirms one-day technical bounce
